steps.title: Build the total cost
steps.content: Each container costs $1.87, so $c$ containers cost $1.87c$ dollars. Each roll of tape costs $2.40, so $t$ rolls cost $2.40t$ dollars. Total spent: $1.87c + 2.40t$.

steps.title: Apply the budget
steps.content: Leo has $15, which is the maximum he can spend. The total cost must be at most $15$, giving $1.87c + 2.40t \le 15$.

steps.title: Test the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $1.87c + 2.40t \le 15$: Correct coefficients (containers at $1.87$, tape at $2.40$) and the correct $\le$ direction for a spending limit. Works.
  • Choice B — $1.87c + 2.40t \ge 15$: Coefficients are right, but $\ge 15$ would force Leo to spend at least $15. He only has $15. Eliminate.
  • Choice C — $2.40c + 1.87t \le 15$: The coefficients on $c$ and $t$ are swapped, charging the tape price for containers. Eliminate.
  • Choice D — $2.40c + 1.87t \ge 15$: Both the coefficients and the inequality direction are wrong. Eliminate.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
